title: ApplyPatchFileMetadata schema change
description: Tool-return type for apply_patch narrows. No persistent storage schema migration — SQLite parts.payload_json is a JSON blob; consumers must tolerate optional fields. New sessions emit narrowed shape; legacy sessions remain readable.

filePath.description: Absolute path to the touched file.

diff.description: Unified hunk-format diff for this file.

before.description: REMOVED. Full file body before the patch. Caused ~388 KB per call for a 388 KB file.

after.description: REMOVED. Full file body after the patch. Same waste as before.

diff.description: Unified hunk-format diff for this file. Single source of patch information consumed by UI and humans.

files.description: Per-file metadata; entries follow the narrowed shape above.

diff.description: Aggregate hunk-format diff across all touched files.

storageMigration.rationale: SQLite parts.payload_json is JSON-blob storage; no schema column changes. UI must accept both shapes. New rows omit before/after; old rows continue to carry them. Dream-pruner continues to strip them from legacy rows opportunistically.
storageMigration.rollback: Re-add before/after fields to the apply_patch return statement. No data backfill needed — old sessions never lost their bodies; new sessions never had them.

parts.note: payload_json column accepts both old and new ApplyPatchFileMetadata shapes; no migration.
